name: judicial_institutional_trust
description: Perceived legitimacy, fairness, and trustworthiness of formal judicial institutions including courts, judges, and magistrates. Captures whether respondents believe the justice system operates impartially and effectively.

name: legal_cynicism_extrajudicial_justice
description: Disposition to accept or endorse bypassing formal legal processes, including tolerance for extrajudicial punishment by communities or lethal force by security forces instead of due process. Reflects distrust in the law's capacity to deliver justice.

name: police_trust_and_legitimacy
description: Respondent's trust in and respect for police, and perception of whether police act fairly and professionally. Combines affective trust ratings with evaluative judgments about police conduct.

name: legal_self_efficacy_and_access
description: Respondent's perceived capacity to navigate the legal system, including knowledge of rights, willingness to use courts, access to legal representation, and beliefs about the utility of formal legal recourse.

name: law_compliance_motivation
description: The normative and instrumental reasons underlying why individuals obey laws, and their self-assessed versus perceived law-abidingness of citizens and government officials. Captures the motivational basis of legal compliance.
